name: Chimneysweeps
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Semisweet chocolate; melted and cooled
1/4 cup Blanched almonds; toasted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Mimi Markofsky
Preparation Time: 0:45
1. Place rack in center of oven.
2. Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
3. Line baking sheets with parchment paper and lightly butter the parchment (if cooking spray is available, spray the paper).
4. In a food processor fitted with the steel blade, grind 1 cup blanched almonds and 2 tablespoons sugar to a fine texture.
5. Add 1 egg white to the food processor and blend for 10 seconds.
6. Add half of the remaining sugar and blend for 10 seconds.
7. Add the remaining sugar and blend for another 10 seconds.
8. Transfer the mixture to a medium bowl.
9. Mix in the melted chocolate, chopped almonds, and cocoa powder.
10. Using a tablespoon, drop the batter onto the prepared sheets, leaving 1-1/2 inches between each cookie.
11. Moisten fingertips with cold water and shape the dough into 3/4 inch high 1-1/4 inch wide mounds.
12. Set a whole almond into the center of each mound, pointed end up.
13. Bake until the cookies are dry and just firm on the outside but soft in the center, about 12-15 minutes.
14. Let set on baking sheet for 5 minutes.
15. Transfer to a rack and cool.
